title: alchemilla alpina subsp. asterophylla
intro: Alchemilla alpina subsp. asterophylla, also known as the lady's mantle subalpine or alpine lady's mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a perennial herb with serrated leaves and small white flowers, traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and astringent effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and promote wound healing, making it a popular remedy for menstrual issues and skin conditions. Alchemilla alpina subsp. asterophylla can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, often used to treat menstrual disorders, skin irritations, and minor wounds.
